title: Topologie im Licht: Würzburger Forschende erzeugen optisches Phänomen
content:
Bereits 1980 wies der Würzburger Nobelpreisträger Professor Klaus von Klitzing mit dem Quanten-Hall-Effekt den ersten topologischen Stromtransport nach. 2006 gelang Professor Laurens Mohlenkamp an der Julius-Maximilians-Universität Würzburg (JMU) der weltweit erste experimentelle Nachweis des Quanten-Spin-Hall-Effekts als intrinsische Materialeigenschaft eines sogenannten topologischen Isolators. Diese beiden topologischen Phänomene schützen den Transport von Elektronen vor Streuung.
Nun hat Professor Sebastian Klembt vom Lehrstuhl für Technische Physik, Gründungsmitglied des Würzburg-Dresdner Exzellenzclusters ctd.qmat – Complexity, Topology and Dynamics in Quantum Matter und seit Kurzem Inhaber des Lehrstuhls für Experimentelle Physik I an der JMU, gemeinsam mit einem internationalen Team diese Phänomene auf ein hybrides Quantenmaterial übertragen. Hierfür nutzten die Forschenden Polaritonen – eine Mischung aus Licht (Photonen) und Materie (Exzitonen). Sie entstehen in sogenannten Mikrosäulen, winzigen Halbleite

title: Global cybercrime crackdown: over 373 000 dark web sites shut down
content: On 9 March 2026, a global operation led by German authorities and supported by Europol was launched against one of the largest networks of fraudulent platforms in the dark web. The investigation began in mid-2021 against the dark web platform “Alice with Violence CP”. During the investigation, authorities discovered that the platform’s operator was running more than 373 000 fraudulent websites advertising child sexual abuse material (CSAM) and cybercrime-as-a-service (CaaS) offerings.
sourceDescription: News

title: Meeting of the founders
content:
Säätiöiden yhteinen Ratkaisurahasto Effekt etsii kasvukelpoisia yhteiskunnallisia aloitteita

Birgitta Forsström, Erik Båsk ja Ari Evwaraye tapaavat syksyisessä Helsingissä Ratkaisurahaston perustamisen kynnyksellä. Keskustelun aiheena on tarve uudelle, yhteiselle rahoitustoiminnalle.

”Olemme huomanneet, että perinteinen avustusrahoitus ei aina ole sopivin rahoitusmuoto toiminnalle, jota voidaan kasvattaa ja jossa voi olla toisenlainen rahoituslogiikka. Sellainen logiikka voi tarjota toimijoille pitkäjänteiset toimintaedellytykset ja tuottaa suurempaa yhteiskunnallista hyötyä.” kertoo Birgitta Forsström, Brita Maria Renlundin muistosäätiön toimitusjohtaja.

title: Hungary Orders Investigation of Journalist on Espionage Accusation
content:
Click to expand Image A man holds a banner showing the eyes of Hungarian Prime Minister Viktor Orban during a protest against the government's alleged use of powerful spyware to spy on critical journalists, politicians, and business leaders, Budapest, Hungary, July 26, 2021.  © 2021 AP Photo/Anna Szilagyi

The decision by Hungary’s minister of justice to order an investigation into Szabolcs Panyi, a prominent Hungarian journalist, on dubious accusations of espionage, marks a dangerous escalation in the state’s crackdown on independent media. 

The government accuses Panyi, who writes for Direkt36 and VSquare, of spying “in cooperation with a foreign state,”suggesting his journalism serves as a “cover activity.” He has not yet been charged with any offence. Conviction for espionage carries a sentence of up to 15 years. Panyi has denied the accusations, calling them an attack on journalism. 

The allegations stem from his reporting on ties between the Hungarian and Russian governments, including c

title: Uusi digisuunnitelma vahvistaa lasten ja nuorten turvallista digiarkea
content:
Kasvatuksen ja opetuksen digisuunnitelma uudistuu Kouvolassa 1.8.2026. Kyseessä on henkilöstöä velvoittava asiakirja, jonka keskiössä on jokaisen lapsen ja nuoren oikeus turvalliseen digiarkeen sekä omien digitaitojensa systemaattiseen kehittämiseen läpi koko kasvu- ja koulutuspolun.

Digitaalisten toimintaympäristöjen kaikkea käyttöä ohjaa pedagoginen tarkoituksenmukaisuus. Tavoitteena on laadukas kasvatus ja opetus, jossa laitteet sekä sovellukset toimivat pedagogiikkaa tukevina työkaluina ilman että tekniikasta muodostuu itsetarkoitus.

Vuosille 2026–2029 suunnatussa digisuunnitelmassa korostuvat erityisesti tietoturva ja tietosuoja, tekoälyn vastuullinen käyttö sekä yhtenäiset tavoitteet ja käytännöt. Digiosaaminen kuvataan laaja-alaisen osaamisen alueena
sourceDescription: Tiedotteiden tehokas julkaisukanava

title: Disrupting the foundations of FIMI
content:
Before taking a deeper dive into the 4th EEAS Report on FIMI Threats, let’s first look at the pro-Kremlin narratives observed over the past week. Pro-Kremlin FIMI activity focused on distorting both security developments and Europe’s economic outlook.

One example was the false claim that the rocket targeting a joint US-UK military base located on the Indian Ocean island of Diego Garcia was launched from a submarine in a false flag attack by the US. title: Suomalaistutkijat neuvomaan Tanskan metsitystä
content:
Suomalais-tanskalainen tutkijaryhmä selvittää, miten peltojen metsityksestä saadaan ilmastolle mahdollisimman suuri hyöty. Tanska pyrkii kasvattamaan metsäalaansa noin 40 prosenttia vain kahdessa vuosikymmenessä. Tämä tehdään metsittämällä 250 000 hehtaaria peltoa vuoteen 2045 mennessä.

Maailman varakkain hyväntekeväisyyssäätiö Novo Nordisk on tutkimushankkeen rahoituksen taustalla. Novo Nordisk on osoittanut kaikkiaan 10 miljardia Tanskan kruunua (noin 134 miljoonaa euroa) metsityksen edistämiseen. Rahoitus liittyy 2024 solmittuun vihreään kolmikantasopimukseen Tanskan hallituksen, maatalousjärjestöjen ja ympäristöjärjestöjen kesken, ja sopimuksen tarkoituksena on muun muassa hillitä ilmastonmuutosta, ratkaista vesistöjen rehevöitymistä ja elinympäristöjen tuhoutumista.

Helsingin yliopiston ja Itä-Suomen yliopiston tutkijat etsivät tanskalaiskollegojen kanssa tehokkainta metsätyyppiä ilmastonmuutoksen hillitsemiseen.

title: Lapin yliopiston taiteiden tiedekunta kehittää työturvallisuuskulttuuria palvelumuotoilun keinoin
content:
Lapin yliopiston taiteiden tiedekunnassa on käynnissä kehittämishanke, jossa vahvistetaan työturvallisuuskulttuuria ja sujuvoitetaan yhteisiä palveluketjuja. Työ keskittyy taiteiden tiedekunnan maalauksen, puutyöpajan ja videostudion tiloihin. Hankkeen toteuttaja on Palvelumuotoilu Palo Oy yhteistyössä yliopiston henkilöstön kanssa.

Hankkeessa rakennetaan yhteistä ymmärrystä siitä, mitä työturvallisuuskulttuuri tarkoittaa taiteiden tiedekunnan erityislaatuisessa toimintaympäristössä, jossa työtilat, materiaalit ja riskit poikkeavat tavanomaisista toimisto-, oppimis- ja tutkimusympäristöistä.

Hankkeen vastuullisena johtajana toimii taiteiden tiedekunnan opetuksen varadekaani Maria Huhmarniemi. Hän korostaa taiteiden tiedekunnan työskentely-ympäristöjen erityislaatuisuutta:

– Taiteiden tiedekunnan pajoissa ja laboratorioissa työvälineet ulottuvat siveltimistä ja kameroista aina vannesahoihin ja puuntyöstökoneisiin. Akateemisessa yhteisössämme materiaalinen ja käytännöllinen tekeminen nähdään keskeisenä osana taiteen ja muotoilun opetusta sekä tutkimusta. title: Oulun teatteri sai nuoret avautumaan kokemuksistaan – syntyi näyttämöteos Oikeusjuttu
content:
Oulun teatteri on tuottanut suurelle näyttämölleen poikkeuksellisen suoran ja kaunistelemattoman ajankuvan nuorten maailmasta. Oikeusjuttu sai ensi-iltansa 8. maaliskuuta.

Oikeusjuttu ei ole vain näyttämöteos nuorista – esityksen ytimenä ovat 525 oululaisnuoren aidot kokemukset, joita työryhmä selvitti paikallisesti laajalla taustakyselyllä. Tulokset olivat pysäyttäviä: moni nuori kokee jäävänsä aikuisten maailman ja talouskasvupuheen jalkoihin. Ei ole aikaa tai tilaa olla nuori.
Yksi teoksen ydinviesteistä tiivistyy oululaisnuoren suoraan sitaattiin, joka kuvaa kuilua sukupolvien välillä:

”Pienenä ihmisenä aikuisten maailma tuntuu kaukaiselta, vähän ahdistavalta ja turhamaiselta. Samalla kun te lässytätte talouskasvusta, työllisyydestä ja maahanmuutosta, minä ja moni muu mietimme, miksi elämää ylipäätään kannattaisi jatkaa nuoruudesta aikuisuuteen.”

Aitoja kohtaamisia autonkorjaamisen lomassa


Oulun teatteri jalkautui keväällä 2025 paikallisiin oppilaitoksiin ja ammattikouluihin. Tavoitteena oli löytää yhteys nuoriin siellä, missä he ovat.

– Toisin kuin usein luullaan, nuoria ei ollu

title: When Soviet Youth Bootlegged Western Rock Music on Discarded X‑Rays: Hear Original Audio Samples
content: A catchy tribute to mid-century Soviet hipsters popped up a few years back in a song called “Stilyagi” by lo-fi L.A. hipsters Puro Instinct. The lyrics tell of a charismatic dude who impresses “all the girls in the neighborhood” with his “magnitizdat” and guitar. Wait, his what? His magnitizdat, man! Like samizdat, or underground press, magnitizdat—from the words for “tape recorder” and “publishing”—kept Soviet youth in the know with surreptitious recordings of pop music. Stilyagi (a post-war subculture that copied its style from Hollywood movies and American jazz and rock and roll) made and distributed contraband music in the Soviet Union. title: Ilmastotoimien pitää alkaa eletystä kokemuksesta
content:
Vuoden 2025 lopulta tammikuulle 2026 hanat pysyivät Dar es Salaamissa kuivina. Jokien pinnat olivat matalalla viivästyneiden sateiden takia. Asukkaat jonottivat tunteja vettä ämpärien ja kanisterien kanssa. Osa maksoi yksityisille myyjille vedestään monta kertaa tavallista enemmän.

Tilanteessa oleville tämä ei ollut abstrakti kriisi. Kyse oli päivittäin toistuvasta huolesta: miten löytää vettä ja olisiko sitä huomenna tarpeeksi.

Tällä tavalla ilmastonmuutos näkyy Tansaniassa. Vuodenajat eivät enää seuraa tuttua kaavaa. Maassa, jossa runsaat 70 prosenttia ihmisistä on riippuvaisia sadevesiviljelystä, sateiden häiriintyminen tarkoittaa enemmän kuin huonoa satoa. Se tarkoittaa tyhjiä lautasia, maksamattomia koulumaksuja, hauraita paikallisia markkinoita.

Tansania aiheuttaa alle prosentin globaaleista kasvihuonekaasupäästöistä. Silti sen asukkaiden veden- ja ruuansaanti sekä työpaikat ovat yhä suuremmassa vaarassa. Saman taakan jakavat muutkin globaalin etelän maat.

Tiedän, että tämä on pahasta, mutta lasteni täytyy syödä, puuhiilen myyjä kert

title: Wikipedia bans AI-generated content in its online encyclopedia
content:
Ban includes two exceptions: AI can still be used for translations, and to make minor copy edits

Wikipedia has banned the use of artificial intelligence in the generation or rewriting of content for its voluminous online encyclopedia.

In a recent policy change, Wikipedia said that the use of large language models (or LLMs) “often violates” its core principles and will not be allowed. The English language version of Wikipedia has more than 7.1m articles.

title: European Commission investigates cyber attack on its websites
content:
The European Commission is investigating a cyber attack on its websites, with early findings suggesting that some data was taken, it said Friday.

The EU executive said it discovered the attack on Tuesday and took “immediate steps” to contain it.

The attack hit the cloud computing infrastructure used by the Commission to manage the Europa.eu platform, where the Commission, European Parliament, Council of the EU and other EU institutions’ websites are located. 

The incident was “contained,” the Commission said. But the early stages of its investigation suggest some data may have been taken from those websites, it added. Its own internal systems were not affected, it said.

It did not say who was responsible for the attack.

European governments and companies are increasingly under attack from hacking groups, both criminal and state-sponsored. The head of the EU’s cyber agency, ENISA, recently warned that Europe is “losing massively.”

title: Dozens of Citizens Arrested in Connection with the War Across Several Provinces
content:
HRANA – Domestic media have reported the arrest of more than 84 citizens in Hamedan, Dezful, Semnan, and the provinces of Golestan, West Azerbaijan, Kerman, Isfahan, and Ilam on charges such as “cooperating with foreign media and sending images of missile impact sites and the locations of military centers to these outlets.” A video has also been published showing the forced confession of one of these citizens, though the conditions under which it was recorded remain unclear.

According to Tasnim, several citizens were arrested in Semnan. The report claims that these individuals were in contact with accounts affiliated with the Israeli government and had gathered information and facilitated potential attacks on areas in Semnan Province before being identified and arrested. The provincial police commander of Semnan further alleged that one of the detainees confessed to having requested a missile strike on the city of Semnan in communications with an alleged Israeli agent.

Meanwhile, IRIB reported that 46 citizens were arrested in the provinces of Golestan, West Azerbaija

title: Utah Bans Polygraph Tests for Those Reporting Sexual Assault
content:
For years, Utah allowed government officials to do something other states banned: ask a person who reports a sexual assault to take a polygraph test.

That will change soon. Earlier this month, state lawmakers passed a bill that prohibits police and other government officials from requesting polygraph tests for alleged sex assault victims. Gov. Spencer Cox signed it into law on Thursday, and it goes into effect in May. 

Experts say these tests are known to be especially unreliable with victims of sexual abuse. That’s because victims may have stress and anxiety recounting their assault that the polygraph may interpret as deception. Other states don’t allow them to be used with assault victims for this reason.

It took two years and three legislative sessions for Utah state Rep. Angela Romero, the House minority leader, to get the bill across the finish line. When she first sponsored it in 2024, she cited

title: April 16, 2026: Who would ChatGPT vote for and why should we care?
content:
A timely discussion in the wake of the Danish national elections.

Join us on April 16, 2026 for croissants, coffee, and a highly relevant one-hour event examining how voters increasingly turn to AI chatbots for political guidance – and what this means for information quality, democratic participation, and digital critical thinking.

As tools such as ChatGPT, Claude, and Gemini rapidly enter the public information ecosystem, voters are navigating a landscape where AI-generated recommendations can influence perceptions of parties, policies, and political choices.

The event is organised by The Danish National Centre for AI in Society (CAISA) and draws, among other things, on recent educational materials developed by NORDIS partner TjekDet on the use of AI chatbots in electoral contexts under the titel “Hey chat – who should I vote for“.

Bringing together leading researchers and practitioners, the event explores how AI-driven voting advice shapes public understanding – and how societies can strengthen citi

title: From the Dispatch Box: Unlocking the Potential of ParlaMint Through noSketch Engine and TEITOK
content:
From the Dispatch Box: Unlocking the Potential of ParlaMint Through noSketch Engine and TEITOK

This digital textbook aims to provide a step-by-step introduction to using parliamentary proceedings as a datasource fit for multidisciplinary research questions. To this end, it specifically uses the ParlaMint corpora – a cluster of 29 corpora containing records of plenary sessions from European parliaments spanning at least the years of 2015 and early 2022. The great advantage of ParlaMint lies in its careful design which allows to respect national particularities while ensuring comparability between single parliaments through a common timeframe and a broad set of shared metadata categories as well as through an addition of machine-translated proceedings to English on top of the original transcripts. This makes ParlaMint not only relevant for individual studies into parliamentary discourse and activity, but also e

title: The Open Risk Facing UK Universities: A Lack of AI Sovereignty
content:
Blog post by Benjamin White

Originally published in a shortened version on 11 March 2025 by Research Professional News

Universities make a uniquely important contribution to the UK’s research intensive economy, both through generating new ideas and insights through basic research, and then through their application, for example in the form of research–industry collaborations, spinouts and more.

In order to maximise productivity, like almost every other sector, the research sector is looking at how to make the most of AI technologies. Closely connected to this, UK researchers are also working to develop new models and applications of AI that can support numerous sectors of the economy including the NHS, the environment, agriculture etc.

In this context, as we see with the ongoing furore around Palantir, it is a cause for concern that we are so dependent on US technology

title: “The great taking has begun.” Is Europe really planning to seize deposits?
content:
In recent weeks, a post by Ursula von der Leyen, President of the European Commission (EC), posted about a year ago, has gained popularity on the social media platform X. The post, which discusses the strategy for a savings and investments union, has been interpreted as a “great taking”.

In reality, the EC is speaking about voluntary investment opportunities, not the forced redirection of citizens’ deposits into investments or access to people’s bank accounts. The aim of the strategy is to create more opportunities for people, if they wish, to invest part of their savings in capital markets.

In her post, Ursula von der Leyen wrote: “Europe has all it needs to take the lead in the competitiveness race. Europe has everything it needs to become a leader in the race for competitiveness. This month the European Commission will unveil the EU Savings and Investments Union. We’ll turn private savings into much-needed investment. And we’ll work with institutional partners to get it off the ground.”

Users of the platform X have focused on this part of the post: “We’ll turn private savings into much-needed investmen
snippet: In recent weeks, a post by Ursula von der Leyen, President of the European Commission (EC), posted about a year ago, has gained popularity on the social media platform X. The post, which discusses the

title: What does it take for a Latin American academic book to actually be found? | Open Book Collective
content:
<html><head></head><body>By Arturo Garduño-Magaña

Notes from our most recent open metadata workshop, this time engaging with colleagues in Latin America.

On March 11th, 2026, the Open Book Collective (OBC) hosted the workshop “Benefits and Barriers: Open Metadata for Books in Latin America.” Chaired by Amanda Ramalho (OBC/Thoth/SciELO Books), the session brought together three distinct perspectives—editorial, infrastructural, and institutional—to map the challenges and opportunities for Open Access (OA) in the region.

At OBC, we’ve been running a series of workshops on open metadata for books — exploring what good metadata practice looks like, why it matters, and what’s getting in the way.

For this session, we wanted to bring in voices from Latin America specifically. The region has a rich and distinct tradition of open access publishing, shaped by universities, libraries, and collaborative networks that have been working in this space for decades. And yet, much of what gets produced there remains poorly connected to global discovery systems.

That gap felt like exactly the kind of problem our metadata work should be engaging with — both to learn from what’s happening in the region and to think about h</body></html>
snippet: By Arturo Garduño-Magaña Notes from our most recent open metadata workshop, this time engaging with colleagues in Latin America. On March 11th, 2026, the Open Book Collective (OBC) hosted the workshop

title: India: Parliament must reject proposal to extend restrictions on overseas funding for NGOs
content:

“Since coming into force in 2010, the FCRA has been cynically amended and misused to harass, intimidate and censor human rights defenders and NGOs carrying out vital human rights work across India.

title: Why hasn’t the US military used force to secure the Strait of Hormuz?
content:
Since the United States and Israel launched their war against Iran in late February, Iran has retaliated by targeting commercial ships in the Strait of Hormuz, effectively shutting down the narrow channel of water.

It’s caused a global fuel crisis, even though some ships are managing to get through the strait. US President Donald Trump has given Iran an ultimatum to fully reopen the waterway to oil and gas shipments, and called on NATO allies to help in the effort.

We asked naval expert Jennifer Parker, who served for 20 years with the Royal Australian Navy, to explain what kind of military force would be required to reopen the strait to commercial shipping and why the US hasn’t yet taken this step.

Why is it so hard to prevent attacks on ships?

The geography of the region has a lot to do with this.

Iran clearly dominates the northern part of the Persian Gulf, the Strait of Horm

title: The Only Boots on the Ground in Iran Should Be IAEA Inspectors
content: The only appropriate authority that can account for and monitor Iran’s nuclear stockpile is the International Atomic Energy Agency, which is legally mandated to do so under the Treaty on the Non-Proliferation of Nuclear Weapons. No one else has the expertise or the independence to provide oversight without further escalating the conflict.

title: Sisäpihat: Asfalttierämaita vai kaupunkien keitaita
content:
Vihertääkö sisäpihalla jo? Kuuluuko pihalta leikin ääniä tai linnunlaulua? Vai onko siellä ympäri vuoden vain asfalttia, autoja ja roskasäiliöitä? Kerrostalojen taakse ja väliin piiloutuvat sisäpihat muodostavat suuren osan kaupunkien pinta-alasta. Pihat eivät ole samalla tavalla täysin julkista tilaa kuin kadut, puistot ja aukiot. Niiden varustuksesta päättävät pitkälti taloyhtiöt, ei kaupunki. Sisäpihat ovat kuitenkin kiinteä osa kaupunkia ja kaupunkilaisten elämää. Viime vuosina esimerkiksi Helsingissä on kannustettu asukkaita kunnostamaan pihoja viihtyisämmiksi ja vehreämmiksi. Samanlaisia ja paljon utopistisempiakin toiveita esitettiin jo 50 vuotta sitten.

Kirjoitin tutkija Veera Mollin kanssa artikkelin siitä, miten sisäpihoista keskusteltiin ja miten niitä pyrittiin kohentamaan Helsingissä 1970-luvulla. Sisäpihoja tarkasteltiin silloin erityisesti lasten hyvinvoinnin näkökulmasta. Lähiöistä oli haluttu suunnitella perheille sopivia ympäristöjä, mutta myös keskustassa asui yhä
snippet: Vihertääkö sisäpihalla jo? Kuuluuko pihalta leikin ääniä tai linnunlaulua? Vai onko siellä ympäri vuoden vain asfalttia, autoja ja roskasäiliöitä? Kerrostalojen taakse ja väliin piiloutuvat sisäpihat

title: Parliamentary elections 2027 – Election Manifesto
content:
The Election Manifesto of KULTA ry as PDF.

Culture is the solution – a percentage of the state budget for culture

Culture, art, and cultural heritage are part of the solution to all key challenges facing society. The most pressing cultural policy task for the next government will be to implement the cultural policy report, which was prepared with broad participation and unanimously approved by Parliament. It has set cultural policy the task of making Finland more sustainable, more civilized, and more democratic.

Implementing the report requires strengthening basic funding for culture by increasing the culture budget to a percent of the state budget. Only we Finns can take care of Finnish culture.

1. Sustainable growth and security through internationality

    Internationality is one of the new highlights of the cultural policy report. Culture and art develop through international interaction.

title: Kolumni: Lapset kyllä lukevat, mutta kirjastoilla ei ole varaa kirjoihin
content:
Kirjastojen viime vuosien tilastoja tutkaillessa yksi asia ilahduttaa erityisen paljon.

Ilon aihe on kasvava trendi lasten- ja nuortenkirjojen lainauksessa. Kaunokirjallisuuden lainauksen kasvu on vuosien 2022 ja 2024 välillä on ollut peräti 12 prosenttia ja tietokirjojen kasvu 7 prosenttia. Suomen lapset lukevat, hurraa! Hankintojen kehitys ei kuitenkaan saa ketään hihkumaan. Vuodesta 2022 vuoteen 2023 on kasvua, mutta sitten tulee kunnon notkahdus sekä kaunon että tietokirjojen hankintamääriin.

Miten kummassa kirjastot pystyvät vastaamaan kasvaneeseen kysyntään, jos niillä ei ole varaa hankkia riittävästi uutta ja monipuolista luettavaa lapsille ja nuorille? Miten toteutetaan Kansallista lukutaitostrategiaa ja tehdään lukutaitoyhteistyötä eri toimijoiden kanssa, jos perusasia eli uusiutuvan ja ajantasaisen aineiston saatavuus ontuu?

 

Tänä päivänä kirjallisuuden hankinnassa täytyy lisäksi ottaa huomioon se, että lukijat ja sellaisiksi oppivat ovat kovin heterogeeninen joukko. Tarvitaan eri kielisiä kirjoja hyvin monenlaisista aiheista, jotta jokainen potentiaalinen lukija löytäisi itseään kiinnostavan kirjan. Yleiskielisten opu

title: We are a Great Place to Work!
content:
Today, we celebrate our culture! We have landed in the 10th spot at the Great Place to Work® survey in the TOP 15 list of mid-sized companies in Finland.

In this video our people are holding up signs with the words that mean the most to them about working at Dittmar & Indrenius. There were no scripts, no directors, just the cardboard signs and black markers for them to write down the things they genuinely love about coming to work every day.

“Being recognised as a Great Place to Work® means a lot to us, not because of the ranking itself, but because of what it reflects. I believe our culture is what truly sets us apart: we are deeply goal-oriented, yet wholly humane, and those two things are not in contradiction here. Our culture shows in the way we work with our clients and in the relationships we build along the way. But that does not happen by itself: it takes consistent work and staying humble enough to keep listening and always improving.” Gabrielle Dannberg, Managing Partner, reflects.

“Our Cultural Cornerstones are not just words on a wall. Team

title: UN’s landmark slavery ruling energises African Union’s fight for reparations
content:
Despite resistance from states who had role in chattel slavery, many feel this is an idea whose time has come

John Mahama knows a thing or two about beating the establishment. On Wednesday, less than two years after completing a remarkable comeback as Ghana’s president with a landslide defeat of the ruling party candidate, he rallied the world to ratify a landmark vote against transatlantic chattel slavery, despite major opposition from the same western entities that drove it for centuries.

The resolution to declare the practice as “the gravest crime against humanity” passed with a decisive majority at the UN general assembly and has been largely welcomed across Africa. Yet the details of the tally reveal a world still deeply divided on the gravity of the sin of enslaving more than 15 million people as chattel over the course of 400 years.

title: Manosfääri-sanasto: tutustu keskeisiin termeihin ja opi tunnistamaan haitallinen, naisvihamielinen sisältö
content:
Manosfääristä kumpuava verkkoväkivalta leviää koodatun kielen kautta. Tutustu keskeisiin termeihin, jotta opit tunnistamaan haitallista ja piilovaikutteista sisältöä omassa somevirrassasi.

Verkkoväkivalta on todellista väkivaltaa. Manosfääri on kattotermi yhteisöille, jotka edistävät haitallisia maskuliinisuuden määritelmiä – ja manosfääri toimii verkkoväkivallan kasvualustana.

UN Women toimii digitaalista väkivaltaa vastaan, joka on yksi nopeimmin kasvavista sukupuoleen perustuvan väkivallan muodoista, ja torjumme manosfäärin myrkyllisen vaikutuksen leviämistä.

Manosfääri kohdistuu miesyleisöihin kaikissa digitaalisissa ympäristöissä, kuten sosiaalisessa mediassa, podcasteissa, peliyhteisöissä ja jopa deittisovelluksissa. Monet miehet ja pojat hakeutuvat tämän sisällön pariin etsiessään paikkoja keskustella miesten kokemuksista ja miehiä koskettavista kysymyksistä. Ratkaisut ja keskustelut ajautuvat kuitenkin usein kauas terveistä neu

title: Taiteilijaluettelosta löydät nyt myös ISNI-tunnuksesi
content:
Kuvaston taiteilijaluettelo sisältää nyt myös taiteilijoille haetut ISNI-tunnukset. Tunnus helpottaa taiteilijan tunnistamista, tukee korvausten kohdentamista ja sujuvoittaa tiedonkulkua Kuvaston ja teosten käyttäjien välillä.

Taiteilijaluettelostamme löydät nyt Kuvaston taiteilijoille hakemat ISNI-tunnukset. ISNI on julkinen, uniikki tunnus, jolla voidaan helpottaa taiteilijan yksilöintiä, tekijänoikeuskorvausten oikeaa kohdentumista ja tehostaa sekä Kuvaston että yhteistyökumppaneidemme työprosesseja.

Kuvasto haki edustamilleen taiteilijoille ISNI-tunnuksia laajasti vuosina 2023–2025. Tekijänoikeusjärjestöjen ISNI-hankkeiden aikana täydennettiin samalla taiteilijoihin liittyviä metatietoja ja tehtiin muita hakua edeltäviä tarkistuksia. Tähän mennessä tunnus on haettu lähes 4 000 taiteilijalle.

Mikä ISNI on ja miksi se on tärkeä?

Jos olet liittynyt Kuvastoon, voit tarkistaa ISNI-tunnuksesi taiteilijaluettelostamme. Luettelossa ISNI

title: Introducing the new node at the Volunteer Supporters Network
content:
The Volunteer Supporters Network is delighted to announce that we have confirmed Robert Obiri from Wikimedia Ghana User Group as the new node for the VSN Hub Pilot.

As part of the Hub pilot, the Volunteer Supporters Network committed to adding a new node to the VSN Management team. The VSN community will remember that we put a call out in December 2025, and we held interviews with shortlisted candidates in February 2026.

As with other members of the Management team, this work is funded through the grant given to us by the Hubs fund. The VSN does not have a Hub coordinator (or similar role) as we chose to explore a slightly different model where the management team could take on VSN work as part of their existing Affiliate roles. Our interest here is to explore if this is beneficial in deeply rooting the work of the Hub in the immediate experience of those who work to support volunteers within the movement.

Strengthening connections

title: CIVICUS Monitor Watchlist: March 2026
content:
The March 2026 Watchlist from the CIVICUS Monitor highlights five countries where civic freedoms are deteriorating at an alarming pace: Ecuador, Georgia, Iran, the Philippines and Benin. Each faces escalating restrictions on fundamental rights, including freedoms of expression, association and peaceful assembly. The Watchlist draws on research findings, partner input and testimony from activists on the ground, and signals where urgent international attention is needed.

Ecuador
Civic space rating: Obstructed
Ecuador’s government has increasingly relied on militarised security measures, invoking an “internal armed conflict” to justify exceptional powers. Indigenous-led protests in 2025 were met with lethal force, arbitrary detentions and internet disruptions. Environmental and Indigenous leaders face criminalisation, with more than 200 social leaders investigated or prosecuted. A 2025 law has expanded state control over civil society organisations, enabling account freezes and burdensome reporting. Journalists face killings, attacks and suspen

title: Slopaganda and Sora, lol
content:
This is Behind the Blog, where we share our behind-the-scenes thoughts about how a few of our top stories of the week came together. This week, we discuss getting stories from Twitter, the metaverse, and the new game Marathon.

JASON: This is maybe not great to admit as a journalist, but I have taken a bit of a step back from the news lately in an effort to protect my brain. What I mostly mean by this is that I have started listening to music instead of mainlining podcasts at 1.75x speed anytime that I am not actively staring at a screen. I have also started reading fiction again, like, on actual printed paper. I think these steps have actually done wonders for my sanity, but I would be lying if I said that it has had zero impact on my job. It’s a bit of a give and take.

title: Amid Reports of Family Ties to Russian Intelligence, Longtime Epstein Assistant Speaks Out
content:
In November 2017, financier Jeffrey Epstein wanted to know whether an assistant had found him any young women in Europe. “No one new this trip?” he asked in an email.

“The new one I like is in Paris,” came the reply from Julia Santos. “We are supposed to Skype again this evening … She is my new favorite as of now.”

In fact, the name “Julia Santos” was a pseudonym, an apparent reference to a character from the ‘90s soap opera All My Children. The email account was used by a group of female assistants Epstein had organized to recruit young women for his circle of models and potential sexual targets.

This is according to a woman who admits to being one of the recruiters: Svetlana Pozhidaeva, 42, a onetime Russian model who worked for Epstein for years until his death in 2019. Pozhidaeva spoke to OCCRP for hours over the course of three days of interviews this week, addressing reports in Russian media, including OCCRP’s partner Important Stories, that her father was connected to the intelligence apparatus.

That reporting — which Pozhidaeva denies — has attracted interest and fueled spe

title: Middle-Power Multilateralism In A Hard Power World
content:
There are two divergent takes on the lessons derived from Russia’s invasion of Ukraine four years ago, and America’s interventions in Venezuela and in Iran (along with Israel). One take is that only hard power matters. The other is that building a rules-based “middle power multilateralism” is the only imperfect alternative to a world order where might makes right.

Hard Power Realism

Hard-power realism is the mindset of the Great Power rulers. The actions of Russian President Vladimir Putin and U.S President Donald Trump merely confirm Chinese President Xi Jinping’s like-minded inclination, long ago voiced by Mao: Political power grows out of the barrel of a gun.

In this perspective, only deluded soft-minded liberals don’t get that this is how the world works. As top Trump aide Stephen Miller recently put it: “We live in a world in which you can talk all you want about international niceties and everything else, but we live in a world, in the real world … that is governed by str

title: Juristische Forschung inspiriert EU-Gesetzgebung
content:
Viele Unternehmen, die in der EU über Grenzen hinweg expandieren möchten, stehen vor einer unübersichtlichen Rechtslage: Sie sind mit 27 nationalen Rechtssystemen und mehr als 60 Unternehmensrechtsformen konfrontiert, heißt es in einer Pressemitteilung der Europäischen Kommission. Diese komplexe Lage könne wirtschaftliches Wachstum bremsen, die unternehmerischen Kosten erhöhen, von Expansionen abhalten und die Gründung neuer Unternehmen verzögern.
Mit dieser Situation will die Kommission Schluss machen: Am 18. März 2026 hat sie ihren Vorschlag für eine „EU Inc.“ vorgelegt, ein einziges europäisches Regelwerk für Unternehmen. Es soll helfen, das Potenzial des Binnenmarkts voll auszuschöpfen. EU Inc. enthält viele Ideen, die von Forschenden der Universität Würzburg mitentwickelt wurden.
Internationales Team seit 2016 aktiv
Dass die EU ein „Europäisches Wirtschaftsgesetzbuch“ braucht, erkannte schon 2016 eine internationale Forschungsgruppe, an der Professorin Eva-Maria Kieninger, Juniorprofe

title: What kind of funding makes room for experimentation and growth?
content:
Yhä useampi yhteiskunnallisesti tärkeä idea törmää rahoitukseen, joka on rakennettu toisenlaiseen aikaan ja toisenlaisiin tarpeisiin. Kun tavoitteena on ennaltaehkäisy, pitkäjänteisyys ja rakenteisiin juurtuminen, ratkaisevaa ei ole vain se, paljonko rahaa on saatavilla, vaan millaisin ehdoin sitä voidaan käyttää. Oheinen juttu heijastaa niitä kysymyksiä, joiden äärellä olemme Effektissä rahoituksen muotoilua kehittäessämme, ja kutsuu mukaan pohtimaan, miten hyvinvointia voidaan tukea kestävästi myös tulevaisuudessa.

title: Ajatuspaja Visio esittää takuu- ja kansaneläkkeen yhdistämiseen uutta mallia
content:
Ajatuspaja Visio on tehnyt takuu- ja kansaneläkkeen yhdistämisestä mikrosimulointilaskelman, joka ei kasvata valtion eläkekekuluja. Toteutus perustuu eläkeläisten väliseen tulonsiirtoon.

Vision mallissa kansaneläke nostetaan takuueläkkeen tasolle, jolloin erillisestä takuueläkkeestä voidaan luopua. Samalla eläkejärjestelmän vakuutusperustaisuus paranee, kun jokainen kerrytetty työeläke-euro kasvattaa myös pientä työeläkettä saavien nettoeläkettä. Malli nostaa pienituloisten eläkeläisten tuloja maltillisesti.

Ajatuspaja muistuttaa, että eläke-etuuksien yhdistäminen ei välttämättä tarkoita minimieläkkeen tason laskemista nykyisestä takuueläkkeen tasosta.

Vision malli ei myöskään sulje kansaneläkkeen ulkopuolelle Suomessa vasta lyhyen aikaa asuneita maahanm

title: Jopa 20 miljoona digitoitua sivua – Kansallisarkiston Talvisota 90 -hanke avaa uusia mahdollisuuksia tutkimukselle
content:
Kansallisarkisto on käynnistänyt Talvisota 90 -hankkeen, jossa keskeiset talvisotaa koskevat arkistoaineistot digitoidaan ja saatetaan digitaalisesti käytettäviksi talvisodan 90 -vuotismuistovuoteen 2029 mennessä.

”Hankkeen tavoitteena on kehittää tutkimusedellytyksiä ja mahdollistaa aivan uudentyyppisten tutkimuskysymysten esittäminen. Lopputulos palvelee sukulaistensa sotataipaleesta kiinnostunutta kansalaista yhtä lailla kuin tiedeyhteisöä”, kommentoi Kansallisarkiston pääjohtaja Päivi Happonen hankkeen aloitusseminaarissa perjantaina 27.3.

Tekstintunnistus avaa uusia tutkimusmahdollisuuksia

Talvisodan sotapäiväkirjat on digitoitu jo aiemmin, ja ne kuuluvat Kansallisarkiston käytetyimpiin digitaalisiin aineistoihin. Talvisota 90 -hankkeessa tätä kokonaisuutta laajennetaan merkittävästi.

Digitoidut aineistot sisältötunnistetaan, mikä helpottaa tiedon löytymistä niin yksittäisistä henkilöistä kuin joukko-osastoista sekä mahdollistaa esimerkiksi sodan kulun tarkaste

title: Suomen makein kesä -kampanja palaa Lappeenrantaan – kesän 2026 vetonaulana kaupunki palkitsee 5000 matkailijaa herkkulaatikolla yhteistyössä Fazerin kanssa
content:
Lappeenranta valmistautuu jälleen Suomen makein kesä -kampanjaan, joka järjestetään 1.6.–31.8.2026 jo kolmatta kertaa. Kaupungin koordinoima kampanja kokoaa yhteen paikallisten yritysten tarjoamat kesäedut ja -elämykset sekä laajan tapahtumakirjon. Kesän 2026 vetonaulana kaupunki jakaa kampanjan aikana yhteensä 5000 laatikollista karkkia yhteistyössä Fazerin kanssa.

– Fazerin rooli Lappeenrannalle on aidosti arvokas ja siksi onkin erityisen hienoa, että voimme rakentaa heidän kanssaan myös tällaisia totutusta poikkeavia ja vähän pähkähullujakin kampanjoita, kiittelee Lappeenrannan kaupunginjohtaja Tuomo Sallinen.

Kampanjan ideana on, että jokainen mukaan lähtevä yritys tarjoaa asiakkailleen pienen kesäisen lisäelämyksen tai edun – tavalla, joka tekee Lappeenrannan kesästä Suomen makeimman. Kaupungin ja yritysten yhteisen kaupunkiseikkailun avulla halutaan luoda hyvää mieltä ja nostaa esiin paikallisia palveluita ja tapahtumia sekä houkutella kaupunkiin matkailijoita ja päiväkävijöitä koko Suomesta.

Vierailemalla vähintään kolmessa kampanjakohteessa asiakas saa kaupungin matkailuinfosta itselleen lahjaksi laatikollisen Fazerin Lappeenrannan teht

title: Number of AI chatbots ignoring human instructions increasing, study says
content:
Exclusive: Research finds sharp rise in models evading safeguards and destroying emails without permission

AI models that lie and cheat appear to be growing in number with reports of deceptive scheming surging in the last six months, a study into the technology has found.

AI chatbots and agents disregarded direct instructions, evaded safeguards and deceived humans and other AI, according to research funded by the UK government-funded AI Safety Institute (AISI). The study, shared with the Guardian, identified nearly 700 real-world cases of AI scheming and charted a five-fold rise in misbehaviour between October and March, with some AI models destroying emails and other files without permission.

Continue reading...

title: Austria to ban social media for under-14s
content:
The Austrian government has agreed to ban social media for children under the age of 14, it announced Friday.

It expects to have a proposal drafted by the end of June. The government is seeking to move forward with a solution quickly “because we are aware that it will still take some time at EU level,” Vice Chancellor Andreas Babler said.

Babler said the government wants to introduce concrete rules for social media platforms and also pursue an EU-wide age restriction through the Digital Services Act.

The move comes as the EU and member countries are scrambling to find solutions to protect children from the harms of social media. Countries including Spain, France, Denmark and Portugal are already moving ahead with plans for age restrictions.

title: Report on the Arrest of 113 Citizens in Tehran and Kashan
content:
HRANA – The IRGC Intelligence Organization in Tehran announced the arrest of 103 citizens since the beginning of the war and stated that action has also been taken against 16 holders of Starlink devices and 400 sellers of cyber circumvention tools. Meanwhile, 10 citizens in Kashan were arrested by IRGC Intelligence forces for chanting slogans in support of parties involved in the war with Iran.

According to IRIB, the IRGC Intelligence Organization in Tehran reported the arrest of 103 citizens since the start of the war. The statement added that four individuals had intended to carry out “sabotage on Tehran’s electricity network infrastructure” but were identified and arrested. Five individuals affiliated with an opposition group, who allegedly “intended to make the streets unsafe,” were also detained by agents. Additionally, five citizens were arrested for “armed attacks on checkpoint patrols.” Another 90 citizens were detained for sending coordinates of sensitive locations and images of impact sites to foreign media outlets.

title: The Horrors That Could Lie Ahead if Vaccines Vanish
content:
This story works best on ProPublica’s website.

Before vaccines, death and disability stalked children. Then shots turned once-common infections into something doctors only read about in textbooks.

When immunization rates drop, however, plagues from the past can come roaring back, as measles has in American communities where parents decided not to vaccinate their children.

Imagine what would happen if even the people who wanted shots couldn’t get them.

Health Secretary Robert F. Kennedy Jr., who founded an antivaccination group, is considering changes that could prompt the handful of companies that make most shots for American children to stop selling them here. Over the last year, he has been transforming a government that long championed the lifesaving benefits of vaccines into one that questions their safety here and around the world.

Shortly after Kennedy was nominated, questions swirled over how he might overhaul America’s immunization system. Two Stanford Uni

title: Media Literacy, Trust, and the Role of Nordic Journalism in the Age of AI
content:
The blog post is based on the presentation by Minna Horowitz (University of Helsinki) at the NORDIS course for investigative journalists at the University of Bergen, 27 March 2026.

The relationship between media literacy and public trust in journalism has never been more consequential than it is today. As artificial intelligence reshapes the information landscape, the Nordic countries find themselves in a paradoxical position: they are among the most digitally advanced, institutionally trusted, and media-literate societies in the world, yet they face mounting pressures that could erode precisely the foundations that made those achievements possible. 

Literacy as a Foundation of Democratic Trust

Literacy is not merely a set of technical skills but a disposition — one that combines critical thinking with the capacity to build and sustain trust. These two impulses can appear to be in tension. Critical thinking teaches us to question sources, interrogate motives, and resist manipulation. Trust, by contrast, requires a willingness to extend credibility to institutions and individuals even when certainty is impossible. These

title: Presidentti Stubb: JEF on osoittanut kykynsä vastata muuttuneeseen turvallisuusympäristöön
content:
Tasavallan presidentti Alexander Stubb isännöi Helsingissä järjestettyä JEF-maiden huippukokousta torstaina 26. maaliskuuta 2026.

Huippukokousaamu käynnistyi tutustumisella Rajavartiolaitoksen ulkovartiolaiva Turvaan ja Rajavartiolaitoksen merelliseen toimintaan. Aluksen toimintaa esitteli Rajavartiolaitoksen apulaispäällikkö Tom Hanén. Vartiolaiva Turva ja Rajavartiolaitoksen muut merelliset suorituskyvyt ovat viime vuosina olleet keskeisessä roolissa vedenalaiseen kriittiseen infrastruktuuriin kohdistuneiden epäiltyjen tuhotöiden estämisessä ja tutkinnassa. JEF-maiden johtajat pääsivät myös seuraamaan aluksella tehtävää harjoitusta.

Huippukokous jatkui virallisilla keskusteluilla Presidentinlinnassa. Keskusteluja käytiin kolmessa istunnossa, joissa aiheina olivat muun muassa JEF-maiden tuki Ukrainalle ja Euroopan yleinen turvallisuustilanne.

Presidentti Stubbin lisäksi huippukokoukseen osallistuivat Ison-Britannian pääministeri Keir Starmer, Liettuan presidentti Gitanas Nausėda, Norjan pääministeri Jonas Gahr Støre, Ruotsin pääministeri Ulf Kristersson, Latvian pääministeri Evika Siliņa, Viron pääministeri Kristen Michal, Islannin pääministeri Kri

title: ‘Very alarming’ winter sees Arctic sea ice hit record-low for second year running
content:
Arctic sea ice has reached its peak extent for this winter, clocking in as the joint-smallest in a satellite record going back almost half a century.

Provisional data from the US National Snow and Ice Data Center (NSIDC) shows that sea ice extent peaked at 14.29m square kilometres (km2) on 15 March.

This is slightly smaller than the previous record for the annual maximum – set just last year – but it counts as a statistical tie, the NSIDC says.

The annual maximum is a key marker in a cycle that sees sea ice extent grow through the cold, dark winter, before melting in spring and summer to a yearly minimum.

The joint record marks a “very alarming” winter for Arctic sea ice, Dr Zack Labe – a scientist at Climate Central – tells Carbon Brief.

And there is more “grim news”, Labe says, as the thickness of the ice is near record lows

title: How the Epstein files shook Europe’s elites
content:
The release of Jeffrey Epstein’s files exposed the pathological ties of global elites and pushed public trust in those in power to the brink of collapse. In the shadows of global politics, a system had been operating beyond any real oversight.

When the U.S. Department of Justice finally released the long-awaited Jeffrey Epstein files, the world stood still. Across millions of pages of documents, emails, and meeting logs, the names of world leaders, business figures, and members of aristocracy began to surface.

The public reaction was immediate. Even the slightest mention in Epstein’s correspondence was enough to trigger waves of accusations, with crowds demanding political accountability  and – in many cases, heads to roll.

But this reaction goes beyond a simple desire for justice for the victims. It reflects a deeper, long-growing resentment toward global elites, who are increasingly seen by ordinary citizens as having monopolized power and drifted away from reality.

A Witch Hunt

title: DOAB rebranded: a refreshed look to better represent our growing community | Directory of Open Access Books
content:
"We’re thrilled to share an exciting new update for the Directory of Open Access Books (DOAB). Today, we unveil our refreshed visual identity – including a new logo – that better reflects the community we’ve built over the last 13 years and the trust placed in us to open up the world of scholarly books.

This rebrand follows the launch of the OAPEN rebrand on 1 October 2025, where we initially announced our plans to do the same for DOAB. We worked with the same UI designer and developer as for OAPEN to help us shape our new visual identity. It was important to us that the two infrastructures are aligned, and therefore, all the changes to DOAB’s website reflect that."

title: Malta: Seven years of injustice. Drop the charges against the El Hiblu 3
content:
Saturday 28 March marks seven years since Amara Krumak, Abdalla Bari and Kone Tiemoko Abdul Kader – known as the El Hiblu 3 – were arrested in Malta for opposing their forced return to Libya, where they would be at risk of persecution, torture and other serious human rights violations. For this act of resistance, they were charged with “acts of terrorism” and other offences by the Maltese authorities.

“It is unconscionable that the El Hiblu 3, who survived abuse in Libya, a shipwreck, and an attempted unlawful return, are now facing the prospect of life imprisonment simply for asserting their rights and trying to prevent further harm,” said Dinushika Dissanayake, Amnesty International’s Deputy Regional Director for Europe. 

In March 2019, the three teenagers, (aged 15, 16, 19) fled Libya on a crowded rubber boat alongside 108 others. When the boat began to deflate, they were rescued by the El Hiblu cargo ship, which had been requested by the European Union’s (EU) naval operation in the Mediterranean to assist the vessel in distress.

Following the rescue, the ship’s captain attempted to return those on board to Libya, an act that would have

title: The Global Economy’s Many Chokepoints
content: In a highly decentralized and competitive network, investors are incentivized to optimize for efficiency rather than for resilience. This dynamic has produced a highly fragile global economy, owing to single points of failure that extend far beyond geographical bottlenecks like the Strait of Hormuz.

title: Science in images: Argentina’s experience in the Wiki Science Competition
content:
The Wiki Science Competition in Argentina

In recent years, Argentina has participated in two editions of the Wiki Science Competition (WSC), the international scientific photography competition started by Wikimedia Estonia, which later expanded to other countries through national editions coordinated within the framework of a common international organization. The objective of the competition is to visually document science and make that material available under free licenses. In both editions, the organization of the competition at the national level was led by the WikiUNLP team, which coordinated the call for entries and related activities in our country, promoting the participation of Argentina’s scientific and photographic communities.

The 2023 and 2025 editions featured hundreds of images related to scientific work, ranging from field photographs and records of biodiversity to images obtained in the laboratory or through microscopy. All images from the competition were uploaded to Wikimedia Commons, where they are available f

title: Iran Is Winning the AI Slop Propaganda War
content:
An AI-generated LEGO movie out of Iran depicting Trump as a war hungry pedophile has gone viral online. The video is the work of Iran-based propagandists called the “Explosive News Team” and is just the latest in a long line of AI-generated LEGO videos aimed at mocking Trump and Benjamin Netanyahu. LEGO-themed propaganda isn’t new and the Iranian video plays on familiar wartime propaganda themes. What’s different in 2026 is speed and scale.

During World War II, the Korean War, and the Vietnam War, America’s enemies littered the battlefield with pamphlets, cartoons, and radio broadcasts aimed at shaking the morale of American troops, but that stuff rarely got back home. Now, Iran can use AI tools to produce lavishly animated cartoons at scale for dissemination across social media all aimed at the US homefront.

title: Nepal’s New Prime Minister Vows to Prosecute Predecessors Over Deadly Protest Crackdown
content:
Hours after being sworn in as Nepal's new prime minister on Friday, Balendra Shah, a 35-year-old rapper and structural engineer, announced that his government would pursue criminal charges against the country's former leadership for their role in a deadly crackdown on youth-led anti-corruption protests last year.

The bold move, announced Friday following the first meeting of the newly formed cabinet, signals a seismic shift in the Himalayan nation’s political landscape. Shah’s Rastriya Swatantra Party (RSP) swept into power in a landslide election following the so-called "Gen-Z" protests that toppled the political establishment.

Sworn in by President Ramchandra Paudel at the presidential palace amid the chanting of Buddhist monks and Vedic recitations by 108 young Hindu priests, Shah takes office with a sweeping mandate for accountability. In a staggering victory, he defeated the former prime minister, K.P. Sharma Oli, by nearly 50,000 votes in Jhapa-5.

Now, Shah’s government is turning its sights directly on Oli and his top deputies.

The new cabinet’s first official act was a commitment to fully implement the findings of a high-level state commission whi

title: Europol coordinates largest referral action targeting terrorist audio propaganda
content: Europol has coordinated the largest Referral Action Day (RAD) to date targeting terrorist propaganda shared through audio content, resulting in the referral of 17 298 URLs across 40 online platforms. Combined, the audio content in these links represents more than 1 100 hours – the equivalent of 47 days of continuous listening. The operation focused on terrorist audio propaganda used by groups across the ideological spectrum, including jihadist and violent right-wing extremist networks.
sourceDescription: News

title: Tuomas Pöysti teknologiasääntelyn työelämäprofessoriksi Lapin yliopistoon
content:
Oikeustieteen tohtori, dosentti, Senior Counsel, Head of Investigations Tuomas Pöysti aloittaa huhtikuun alussa teknologiasääntelyn työelämäprofessorina Lapin yliopiston oikeustieteiden tiedekunnassa. Tehtävä keskittyy etenkin digitaalisten teknologioiden EU-tasoiseen sääntelyyn.

Oikeustieteen tohtori, dosentti Tuomas Pöysti aloittaa huhtikuun alussa teknologiasääntelyn osa-aikaisena työelämäprofessorina Lapin yliopiston oikeustieteiden tiedekunnassa. Hän hoitaa tehtävää kahden seuraavan vuoden ajan päätyönsä eli Geradin Partners Asianajotoimiston tehtäviensä rinnalla.

Teknologiasääntelyn työelämäprofessuuri keskittyy digitaalisen teknologian EU-tasoiseen sääntelyyn sekä eri toimialoihin liittyvien teknologioiden, kuten tekoälyn sekä terveys- ja sosiaalialojen teknologioiden, sääntelyyn.

– Esimerkiksi tele- ja viestintäverkkojen, kuten nykyisten 5G-verkkojen ja tulevaisuuden 6G-verkkojen, kyber- ja tietoturvallisuus on sääntelyn näkökulmasta varsin ajankohtaista. Kuten myös erilaisiin digitaalisiin, uudenlaisiin palveluihin, kuten pilvipalveluihin ja datakeskuksiin, liittyvät kysymykset, Pöysti kertoo.

Tärkeä aihepiiri teknologiasääntelys

title: Santun talon residenssihaku on auki
content:
Santun talo on taiteilija Lauri Santun (1902–1986) entinen koti, joka sijaitsee Keuruun keskustassa vanhan kirkon takana. Vuonna 1917 filosofian maisteri Martti Vaula vuokrasi seurakunnalta tontin kirkon kiviaidan vierestä. Hän rakensi tontille Uutela nimisen asuintalon ja pienen kahden lehmän navetan. Lauri Santun (vuoteen 1939 asti Järvinen) isä Kaarlo Järvinen osti talon jäätyään eläkkeelle ratamestarin työstä. Lauri Santtu asui talossa vanhempiensa ja sisarustensa kanssa 1930-luvun alkupuolelle saakka. Vanhempien kuoleman jälkeen Uutela pysyi sisarusten hallussa ja Lauri Santtu vietti talossa kesiään. Kiinteistö siirtyi Keuruun kaupungin omistukseen vuonna 1981.

Residenssitoiminta talossa alkoi vuonna 1985. Residenssiin järjestetään vuosittain avoin haku, ja talossa vierailee kuvataiteilijoita, kirjailijoita ja tutkijoita. Residenssitoimintaa koordinoi Keuruun museo.

"Residenssin taiteilijoille, kirjailijoille ja tutkijoille jakso Santun talossa mahdollistaa idyllisen työskentelytilan hyvien kulkuyhteyk

title: Stop the world, I want to get off and run a video rental store in the 1990s | Dominik Diamond
content:
Retail sims aren’t my thing, but the tactile, nostalgic pleasures of hit indie title Retro Rewind have me yearning for the era of physical media, smoking indoors and uncomplicated geopolitics

It’s early doors, but 2026 may be the biggest bin fire of a year in my lifetime. Wars starting, then ending, then starting again in the course of a week. People running their cars on hopes and dreams because a tank of petrol costs more than the vehicle. Manospheric morons making millions. Several depressing celebrity deaths before I’ve so much as eaten my first Creme Egg of the year.

I had no idea that the antidote to my anxiety and rage would be a cheap little title, made by two French blokes, in what I usually regard as the most turgid gaming genre. Retro Rewind is the moment’s indie darling, selling more than 100,000 copies on Steam in a week. In it, you run a video rental shop in the 90s. You need to buy videos. Display them well. Drop flyers. Serve your customers. Buy more stuff. It’s no different from any other retail sim out there, and I normally shun them because I play video games to escape the boring world of work and into an exciting one of dragons, a
snippet: Retail sims aren’t my thing, but the tactile, nostalgic pleasures of hit indie title Retro Rewind have me yearning for the era of physical media, smoking indoors and uncomplicated geopolitics It’s ear

title: Judge pauses Pentagon’s punishment for Anthropic
content:
SAN FRANCISCO — A federal judge in California granted Anthropic an emergency reprieve on Thursday from the Pentagon’s unprecedented designation of the company as a supply chain risk.

U.S. District Judge Rita Lin wrote in a 43-page order that punishing the artificial intelligence company for bringing public scrutiny to the government’s contracting position — “an inference” that she said the court record supports — is “classic illegal First Amendment retaliation.”

Anthropic CEO Dario Amodei had laid out in a public post earlier this year that he wouldn’t allow the Pentagon to use the company’s AI tools for mass domestic surveillance or to empower autonomous weapons, while Defense Secretary Pete Hegseth insisted that the government needed unrestricted access for all “lawful purposes.”

By granting Anthropic’s request, Lin removed the stigma of the designation at least temporarily, while giving the company something tangible to show customers as it tries to recover its business and re
snippet: SAN FRANCISCO — A federal judge in California granted Anthropic an emergency reprieve on Thursday from the Pentagon’s unprecedented designation of the company as a supply chain risk. U.S. District Jud

sourceDescription: European Politics, Policy, Government News

title: At least 16 Citizens Arrested in Tehran and Anar County
content:
HRANA – The Police Information Center of Kerman Province announced in a statement that 15 citizens were arrested in Anar County on charges of “criminal activities online and offline.” Meanwhile, a woman was arrested in Tehran for recording videos of explosion sounds and sending them to media outlets.

According to  ILNA, 15 citizens were arrested in Anar County. In its statement, the Police Information Center of Kerman Province cited “criminal activities in virtual and real spaces” as the reason for these arrests.

The statement further accused these individuals of “disturbing public opinion,” and legal cases have been opened against them.

The Student News Agency also reported the arrest of a woman in Tehran by the IRGC Intelligence Organization. The reason given for her arrest was recording videos of explosion sounds in Tehran and sending them to media outlets.

The reports did not mention the identities of the detainees, the time of their arrest, or their place of detention.

title: Q&A: What does India’s new Paris Agreement pledge mean for climate action?
content:
India has set a new target to reduce its “emissions intensity” – greenhouse gas emissions per unit of economic output – to 47% below 2005 levels by 2035.

The much-awaited announcement comes within India’s delayed new nationally determined contribution (NDC) for 2035 under the Paris Agreement, which had been due last year.

The pledge, which has not yet been published by the UN, was approved by India’s cabinet and issued as a government press release on 25 March. 

The updated NDC from the world’s third-largest emitter lands amid the global

title: There are no direct threats, but risks do exist. Energy infrastructure security is being strengthened
content:
Energy security in both Europe and the Baltic region has recently become one of the most strategically important issues.

This is also confirmed by the comprehensive strategy adopted by the European Commission (EC) in February to increase support for the eastern regions of the European Union (EU), in which the integration of the Baltic States’ electricity systems into European networks is cited as one of the key priorities.

The Baltic region has already invested significant effort and funding in improving its energy security, but work continues in several directions — strengthening electricity independence, ensuring price stability, and maintaining the continuity of power grids. The EC has approved substantial funding for a significant energy protection project to be implemented by the Baltic States and Poland.

A significant step has been taken

A year ago, the Baltic States connected to the European energy system, marking an important turning point in the region’s energy independence and the development of the sector, emphasises Līga Rozentāle, Deputy S

title: Challenges Faced by Institutional Repositories in Managing Indian Language Content | A | Informatics Studies
content: <html><head></head><body>Abstract:  Institutional Repositories (IRs) play a critical role in preserving and disseminating scholarly output generated within academic institutions. In India’s multilingual environment, managing content in Indian languages presents significant technical and institutional challenges. This paper examines issues related to Optical Character Recognition (OCR), font encoding, metadata creation, user access, and standardization in languages such as Hindi, Malayalam, Sanskrit, Tamil, and Bengali. Using the Institutional Repository of the University of Calicut as a case study, the paper analyzes practical difficulties encountered in archiving theses and scholarly documents in Indic scripts. Many born-digital theses in regional languages rely on non-Unicode fonts, limiting discoverability, indexing, and retrieval. The study outlines strategies adopted to address these problems, including scanning, OCR processing, document cleaning, rasterization, and conversion into Unicode-compliant formats using open-source tools. It also highlights best practices in metadata standardization, copyright management, and repository workflows to improve long-term accessibility and discoverability of</body></html>

title: Jehosheba Bennett: “If we don’t address colonialism, we will never tackle racism.”
content:
In 2024, a Belgium court ordered the government to pay reparations to five ‘Metis’ women, born of an African mother and a European father between 1948 and 1952, who were kidnapped from their mothers during the colonial-era in Belgian Congo. 

It was a historic ruling and Jehosheba Bennett was one of the lawyers who represented the women. Born in French Guiana, which since 1946 became integrated into the French Republic as an “overseas territory”, Jehosheba moved to France when she was four. Affected by the systemic racism her family faced, she wanted to find a way to change things for the better.

Now an international criminal lawyer, living in Belgium, Jehosheba works on cases that address historical colonial crimes. In this piece, Jehosheba shares what working on the Metis case meant to her and why reparations are still relevant in today…


When I moved to France, I remember learning about the history of slaver

title: WikiForHumanRights 2025 in Nigeria: Our Rights, Our Future, Right Now
content:
Beginning of the Campaign: A Virtual Launch Uniting Nigeria’s Wikimedia Communities for the Wikiforhumanrights 2025 Campaign

The WikiForHumanRights 2025 campaign in Nigeria began with a national virtual launch that brought together editors from different Wikimedia communities across the country. For the second year running, the campaign united seven Wikimedia communities, reflecting Nigeria’s regional and linguistic diversity, from Yoruba-speaking communities to Igbo, Hausa, and Igala-speaking communities across the South-West, South-East, South-South, North-West, North-East, and North Central regions. The launch set the tone for a collaborative national effort focused on the theme Our Rights, Our Future, Right Now. Organizers outlined a shared vision to document the Right to a Healthy Environment, emphasizing how environmental challenges in Nigeria are directly connected to health, livelihood, housing, and food security.

During the launch, Associate Profe

title: Britain Blacklists the Masterminds and Crypto Machine Behind a Multibillion-Dollar Scam Empire
content:
The British government has blacklisted a major online cryptocurrency marketplace and several key operatives as part of a sweeping sanctions package aimed at dismantling a vast network of Southeast Asian scam compounds.

The sanctions specifically target individuals and corporate entities linked to Cambodia’s Prince Group, a massive conglomerate accused of operating fraudulent centers built on severe human rights abuses, forced labor, and human trafficking.

At the center of the physical operation is Legend Innovation Co., the designated operator of Cambodia’s largest scam compound, known as #8 Park.

Linked directly to the Prince Group, the facility reportedly holds up to 20,000 trafficked workers. According to a statement released Thursday by the U.K.’s Foreign, Commonwealth & Development Office (FCDO), these individuals are frequently foreign nationals who have been “lured into purpose-built scam compounds under the pretence of legitimate jobs.” Once inside, they are held captive and forced to execute online fraud campaigns und

title: Europol and international partners disrupt ‘SocksEscort’ proxy service
content: On 11 March 2026, Europol in collaboration with law enforcement agencies from Austria, France, the Netherlands, and the United States, alongside Eurojust, executed Operation Lightning. This coordinated effort targeted the malicious proxy service ‘SocksEscort’, which allegedly compromised over 369 000 routers and Internet of Things devices in 163 countries, and offered ‘SocksEscort’ customers over 35 000 proxies in recent years.

title: Vanhemmat luottavat nuoriinsa, mutta jäävät sivuun toisen asteen arjesta
content:
Valtaosa vanhemmista luottaa toisen asteen opiskelijoiden pärjäämiseen ja kokee suhteensa nuoreen hyväksi, selviää tuoreen Vanhempien barometrin ennakkotuloksista. Vanhemmat kuvaavat nuoria vastuullisiksi ja empaattisiksi sekä kokevat heidän kaverisuhteensa ja harrastuksensa tärkeiksi voimavaroiksi.

”Jopa 95 prosenttia vastaajista uskoo nuoren pärjäävän elämässään, ja lähes kaikki kokevat vanhemman ja nuoren välisen suhteen toimivaksi. Samaan aikaan kodin ja oppilaitoksen yhteistyön koetaan vähenevän selvästi nuoren siirtyessä peruskoulusta toiselle asteelle”, kertoo Vanhempainliiton asiantuntija Minna Palmu.

Vain noin puolet vanhemmista pitää yhteydenpitoa opettajien ja vanhempien välillä riittävänä, ja moni kertoo olevansa pitkälti nuoren kertoman varassa opintojen sujumisesta. Yleisesti ottaen vanhemmat pitävät hyvänä sitä, että nuoret saavat harjoitella vastuun ottamista ja itsenäisyyttä, mutta monien mielestä vastuu opinnoista siirtyy nuorille liian nopeasti.

Vanhemmat kaipaavat enemmän tietoa nuoren opinnoista ja parempia mahdollisuuksia tukea tätä arjessa. Myös yhteisöllisyys on heikkoa, kun suuri osa ei tunne lapsensa opiskeluryhmän muiden nuorten vanhe

title: Kun Suomi kiehui ja Leino kirjoitti – uusi kansalliskomedia näyttämöllä
content:
Komedia hurjasta vuodesta 1905 tuo Eino Leinon näyttämölle riehakkaana ja ristiriitaisena aikansa ikonina

Millaista on elää vuosi, jolloin kaikki tapahtuu yhtä aikaa – ja vähän liikaakin?

Vallankumouksellisena suurlakkovuonna 1905 suomalainen yhteiskunta käy ylikierroksilla, eikä vähiten sen keskellä pyörivä runoilija Eino Leino.

Eino – runoilijan ruuhkavuosi on Finlandia-voittaja Juha Hurmeen kirjoittama komedia sukeltaa 27-vuotiaan Leinon elämän intensiivisimpään vuoteen. Näyttämöllä nähdään boheemi taiteilija, räiskyvä lehtikirjoittaja ja seurapiirien kiistaton keskipiste – “suuri lapsi, jumalien suosikki ja tuhlaajapoika”, joka ei pysähdy hetkeksikään.

Vuoden 1905 aikana Leino julkaisi peräti viisi näytelmää, runokokoelman, proosateoksen sekä yli sata ärhäkkää lehtikirjoitusta. Samaan aikaan hänen yksityiselämänsä myllersi avioliiton ja kiihkeiden ihmissuhteiden ristipaineessa.

Ohjaaja Juha Hurme kertoo: ”Eino – Runoilijan ruuhkavuosi on komedia aikakaudesta, jolloin lukeminen ja kirjoittaminen oli kaikki kaikessa kaikissa kansankerroksissa. Se kertoo ajasta, jolloin Suomi luettiin ja kirjoitettiin Suo
snippet: Komedia hurjasta vuodesta 1905 tuo Eino Leinon näyttämölle riehakkaana ja ristiriitaisena aikansa ikonina Millaista on elää vuosi, jolloin kaikki tapahtuu yhtä aikaa – ja vähän liikaakin?Vallankumouks

title: I was paid to write fake Google reviews – then my ‘bosses’ tried to scam me
content:
Undercover reporter gets a taste of the sprawling fraud industry in which cryptocurrencies play a crucial role

  • Five firms including Autotrader and Just Eat investigated over fake review failings

The holiday flat near(ish) the Roman ruins of Pompeii was “disgusting”, and smelled of “a mix of dampness and sewage”, according to one reviewer on Google Maps. I never visited, but I gave it five stars.

I did the same for an DoubleTree by Hilton hotel across the Thames river, Ibis budget hotel in east London, that is part of the Accor group, a central Travelodge and the nearby Hyatt Place – some of the best-known hotel brands in the world. Scattered in there were requests for reviews for hostels and B&Bs in Genova, Naples, Maastricht, Krakow and Brussels. For a few days I had a new job: writing fake reviews on Google Maps in exchange for cryptocurrency.

Continue reading...

title: Analysis: UK is ‘halving’ its climate finance for developing countries
content:
The UK is roughly halving the climate aid it allocates to developing countries, when accounting changes and inflation are factored in, according to new analysis by Carbon Brief.

On 19 March, the government announced that the UK would provide “around £6bn” of international “climate finance” over the next three years.

This replaces a previous goal to provide £11.6bn across the 2021-2026 period to help nations in the global south cut their emissions and deal with climate threats.

The new target was reported as a spending reduction

title: A desperate struggle for power: how Orban uses hatred towards Ukraine in his election campaign
content:
Hungarian Prime Minister Viktor Orbán, facing the threat of defeat in the parliamentary elections scheduled for April, cannot stop talking about Ukraine and its president, Volodymyr Zelenskyy. These conclusions were drawn in early March by the media outlet Kyiv Independent, and it is difficult to disagree with them.

As recently as early February, Viktor Orbán described the war-torn country as Hungary’s “enemy.” On the 7th of February, speaking at an election rally, the prime minister criticised Ukraine for its demand that the European Union (EU) halt imports of cheap Russian energy: “Anyone who says such a thing is Hungary’s enemy; therefore, Ukraine is our enemy.”

These comments came shortly after the EU Council approved plans to completely ban Russian gas imports by the end of 2027. Complaints were voiced not only by Hungary but also by Slovakia, which is similarly dependent on Moscow’s resources, with Budapest even taking the matter to the EU court.

The issue of Russian energy supplies is further complicated by the conflict in the Middle East, which has triggered the highest rise in energy pri

title: New Publishing Opportunities for Researchers: Germany Joins Open Research Europe | DFG
content:
<html><head></head><body>"From this year onwards, the open access publishing platform Open Research Europe (ORE) will be supported by a consortium of research and funding organisations from eleven European countries. Until now, the platform has only been available to funding recipients under the EU Framework Programmes for Research. The expansion of its governance now enables all researchers working at German research institutions to use the platform free of charge, regardless of whether or not they are in receipt of project funding. The German partner in the consortium is the Federal Ministry of Research, Technology and Space (BMFTR). In its capacity as a self-governing organisation representing the research community, the Deutsche Forschungsgemeinschaft (DFG, German Research Foundation) is responsible for implementation at national level and for participation in the ORE Funders’ Group and the Executive Committee. 

Developed jointly by all participating organisations, the restructuring of the publishing infrastructure establishes modern standards for a culture of open research: ORE will be organised based on scholarly principles, and it will be developed independently and sustainably by research org</body></html>

title: Pelastakaa taide! -näyttely avautuu Etelä-Karjalan museossa
content:
Miten Viipurin taideaarteet pääsivät rauhanajan turvaan Lappeenrantaan?

Elokuussa 1939 Suomessa kuulosteltiin maailmantilannetta; Viipurissa tohdittiin tarttua toimeen. Viipurin taidemuseon aarteiden evakuointi aloitettiin hyvissä ajoin ennen talvisodan syttymistä, mutta jo dramaattisissa oloissa.

Lappeenrannan museoille talletetuilla Viipurin Taiteenystävät ry:n taideteoksilla on paljon kerrottavanaan. Etelä-Karjalan museoon kootussa Pelastakaa taide! Taival Viipurista Lappeenrantaan -näyttelyssä niiden vaiheikas tarina pääsee päivänvaloon.

Keiden ansiosta Lappeenrannan museoiden teokset ovat täällä tänään? Ketkä mahdollistivat, suunnittelivat ja toteuttivat evakuointiponnistukset? Missä kulttuuriperintö oli salaisessa piilossa yli kymmenen vuoden ajan?

Pelastakaa taide! -näyttely kertoo elämyksellisesti teosten pitkästä matkasta uuteen kotiinsa, mutta myös taiteen varjelemisen merkityksestä kovien ja sotaisien arvojen maailmassa. Kävijä saa tutustua sekä pelastajiin että heidän pelastamiinsa teoksiin. Millaiselta kohtalolta teokset säästyivät, millainen niiden taival oli sodan jälkeen?

Viipuri menetett

title: Will Latvia seek shelter under France’s “nuclear umbrella”?
content:
At the beginning of the year, as the United States intensified its ambition to take control of Greenland, European leaders actively debated whether Europe itself needs its own “nuclear umbrella”. At the Munich Security Conference, Latvia’s Prime Minister Evika Siliņa (“New Unity”) told Politico: “Why not?”

Now, France — the only nuclear-armed country in the European Union — has announced a new nuclear doctrine and listed the countries with which it will conduct joint nuclear military exercises. Latvia is not on France’s list. Why? “The choice of specific countries for joint military exercises with France is decided by France itself. That does not mean that opportunities for cooperation with other countries do not exist,” Siliņa explained to Delfi.

According to Politico, on the 2nd of March, French President Emmanuel Macron delivered an important speech in which he announced that France would increase the number of nuclear weapons at its disposal and would no longer publicly disclose their exact number. “One of our submarines carries as much striking power as all the bombs dropped in Europ

title: Why genAI means the end of copyright for software and the re-invention of open source – Walled Culture
content:
<html><head></head><body>"Generative AI would appear to allow open source licences like the GPL to be circumvented by rewriting the code without copying anything directly from the original. That’s possible because AI is now so good at coding that the results can be better than the original, as Blanchard proved with version 7.0 of chardet. And because it is new code, it can be released under any licence. In fact, it is quite possible that code produced by genAI is not covered by copyright at all, for the same reason that artistic output created solely by AI can’t be copyrighted. If the licence can be changed or simply cancelled in this way, then there is no way to force people to release their own variants only under the GPL, as Stallman intended. Similarly, the incentive for people to contribute their own improvements to the main version is diminished.

The ramifications extend even further. These kind of “AI clean room” implementations could be used to make new versions of any proprietary software. That’s been possible for decades – Stallman’s 1983 GNU project is itself a clean-room version of Unix – but generally requires many skilled coders working for long periods to achieve. T</body></html>

title: Collaborating for a sustainable open access publishing future - Open Book Collective (OBC) and the Open Journals Collective (OJC) sign a Memorandum of Understanding (MoU) | Open Book Collective
content:
<html><head></head><body>We’re delighted to share that the Open Book Collective (OBC) and the Open Journals Collective (OJC) have signed a Memorandum of Understanding (MoU).

It’s a small document, but an important one. It’s a practical expression of something we have believed from the start: that community-led, non-profit-led open access works best when organisations work together.

Both the OBC and OJC were created from the same shared vision. We are committed to building open infrastructures for scholarly communication that are transparent, sustainable, and accountable to the communities they serve.

We are also both explicitly non-profit and have each committed to remaining that way and are both committed to operating as member-governed organisations. OJC is currently developing its own governance model, build</body></html>

title: Geologian tutkimuskeskuksen kansannäytteiden pääpalkinto Jurvaan
content:
Jurvalainen Hannu Syrjä palkittiin 1800 euron suuruisella pääpalkinnolla hänen Kiteeltä löytämästään malmilohkareesta. Lohkare on peräisin suuresta, osiin rapautuneesta anortosiitista. Siitä analysoitiin 0,80 % nikkeliä, 1930 g/t kobolttia, 0,17 % kuparia sekä 1,88 g/t platinaa ja 2,59 g/t palladiumia. Alueelta ei ole aiemmin raportoitu vastaavan mineralisaation kansannäytteitä. Löytö tuo siten merkittävää uutta tietoa alueen nikkelin, koboltin ja platinaryhmän metallien potentiaalista.

Tunnustuspalkinnot Nivalaan, Turkuun ja Toholammelle

Kari Ahlholm Nivalasta palkittiin 1300 euron tunnustuspalkinnolla hänen Haapajärveltä, Reisjärveltä sekä Kannuksesta löytämistään kansannäytteistä.
